section_path: Understanding your obligations > Obligations based on entity type > Deployer
content:
## Obligation: Deployer obligations for high-risk AI systems

A deployer of a high-risk AI system is subject to the obligations set out in Article 26 of the EU AI Act. While the deployer obligation set is narrower than the provider obligation set, it is substantive and operationally significant: deployers are the actors who actually put high-risk systems into use, and Article 26 is designed to ensure that their use of those systems is informed, monitored, and accountable.

The core duties under Article 26 are: taking appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure the system is used in accordance with the instructions for use provided by the provider; assigning human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training, authority, and support; ensuring that input data is relevant and sufficiently representative for the intended purpose, to the extent the deployer exercises control over the input data; monitoring the operation of the system on the basis of the instructions for use and informing the provider of any serious incident or malfunction; keeping the logs automatically generated by the system to the extent they are under the deployer's control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose and at least six months unless other Union or national law provides otherwise; informing workers' representatives and affected workers before putting a high-risk system into service in the workplace; and, where applicable, cooperating with national competent authorities on any action they take in relation to the system.

Deployers that are public authorities or private entities providing public services face an additional obligation to perform a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment under Article 27 before deploying the high-risk system, except where the system is used in critical infrastructure.

Source: Article 26, Article 27
source_document: FLI EU AI Act Compliance Checker
